steps.title: Use the one-solution condition
steps.content: The line $y=-2.5$ and the parabola $y=x^2+10x+a$ have exactly one distinct real solution only when the line touches the parabola at its vertex.

steps.title: Find the vertex height
steps.content: Complete the square: $x^2+10x+a=(x+5)^2+a-25$. The vertex has $y$-coordinate $a-25$.

steps.title: Set the vertex on the line
